feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Finland replaced artificial playground surfaces with natural elements like mud and soil — and the results surprised even researchers
entry.summary: The goal was to increase children's exposure to environmental microbes through everyday play.

entry.title: US health officials nix publication of a study on Covid vaccine effectiveness
entry.summary: U.S. health officials stopped the publication of a study on whether the Covid-19 vaccine was keeping adults from becoming sick enough to have to go to the hospital

entry.title: NJ warns of possible measles exposures at Newark Airport
entry.summary: A Hudson County resident was diagnosed with measles after returning from an international trip, the state health department said.

entry.title: Even light drinking combined with aging is linked to reduced brain blood flow and thinner tissue
entry.summary: A new study suggests that even low-level drinking over a lifetime may negatively affect brain health. Researchers found that cumulative light drinking, combined with aging, is linked to reduced blood flow and thinner tissue in the brain.

entry.title: Long COVID potentially affects nearly 6 million children in the US. This South Shore teen is one of them.
entry.summary: In children, long COVID symptoms vary by age, possibly because of how the immune system develops.

entry.title: Rectal cancer deaths rising among younger adults, Upstate study finds
entry.summary: The study was conducted at SUNY Upstate Medical University in Syracuse.

entry.title: Scientists Identify The Most Dangerous Time in Life to Gain Weight
entry.summary: A new study suggests it's not just gaining weight that affects our health over a lifetime, but also when we put on the pounds, with weight gain during early adulthood more strongly associated with mortality risk.

entry.title: Hawaii family found a Valley Fever lifeline in Phoenix
entry.summary: Kaha Kelau came to Arizona to become a diesel mechanic. He left with a fungal infection that would trigger a stroke. Nearly 3,000 miles away, his mother found the one man who could help.

entry.title: These 80-year-olds have the memory of 50-year-olds. Scientists now know why
entry.summary: A rare group of adults over 80, known as SuperAgers, are rewriting what we thought was possible for the aging brain. With memory abilities comparable to people decades younger, their brains either resist or withstand the damage typically linked to Alzheimer’s…

entry.title: The Tech Industry Has a Plan to Reanimate the Dead. I Should Know: They Want to Bring Back My Brother.
entry.summary: A new industry has emerged that promises to resurrect the dead—but it completely misunderstands what it means to lose them in the first place.

entry.title: Golden Retriever Wants the Baby’s Food, but It Doesn’t Go as Planned
entry.summary: A golden retriever’s quiet attempt to score an easy snack has the internet laughing after a video captured the exact moment his plan unraveled, right in...

entry.title: Want to Protect Your Brain From Alzheimer's? A 16-Year Study Suggests a Simple Thing to Do Right Now
entry.summary: Maintaining adequate vitamin D levels in midlife may help reduce the risk of Alzheimer’s-related brain changes later in life.

entry.title: Personalized Vaccine For 'Deadliest Major Cancer' Keeps Patients Alive 6 Years Later
entry.summary: At a major cancer research conference in the United States, scientists have shared the latest on an experimental vaccine for an extremely deadly cancer.

entry.title: Evidence of Prehistoric Surgery 30,000 Years Ago Challenges Human History
entry.summary: A study reveals evidence of prehistoric surgery in Borneo, where a young adult survived a leg amputation about 31,000 years ago.

entry.title: Punctuated decline of human cooperation
entry.summary: Cooperation in group lending declines over time due to shifting behavioural motivations, briefly rebounds after loan restarts, then declines faster again, revealing systematic deviations from rational behaviour driving long-term cooperation loss.

entry.title: The age you start regularly watching adult content predicts your future mental health
entry.summary: Researchers have identified three distinct patterns of adult media consumption, revealing that individuals who begin a regular viewing habit at a young age face a higher risk of psychological distress and behavioral struggles later in life.

entry.title: Astrocytes connect specific brain regions through plastic networks
entry.summary: Communication between distant brain regions is mediated by plastic networks of gap junction-coupled astrocytes.

entry.title: There’s a new sex party crasher in town… & he’s drug-resistant and lives in your gut
entry.summary: One gay doctor is calling for more "d*ck washing stations" at parties.

entry.title: A bat coronavirus can enter human cells through a previously unknown gateway
entry.summary: The identification of a receptor that is recognized by a subset of alphacoronaviruses provides insights into ‘spillover’ risk and pandemic potential.

entry.title: Ubiquitination of glycogen and metabolites in cells and tissues
entry.summary: Nature - Ubiquitination of glycogen and metabolites in cells and tissues

entry.title: Focal white matter lesions drive grey matter inflammation and synapse loss
entry.summary: A transient grey matter microgliosis is required for remyelination, the failure of which results in a chronic neuroinflammatory state seen in neurodegenerative disorders.

entry.title: HEPA air purifiers may boost brain power in adults over 40
entry.summary: Using an in-home HEPA purifier for one month spurs a small but significant improvement in brain function in adults age 40 and older. That's the result of a new study we co-authored in the journal Scientific Reports.

entry.title: Oral cancer Bengaluru: Rise in young cases alarms doctors
entry.summary: More young people diagnosed with oral cancer, say doctors

entry.title: 2 Key Things Women over 60 Should Do for Better Gut Health
entry.summary: We asked a gastroenterologist: What are some things that women over 60 can do to benefit their gut health? Here’s what she had to say.

entry.title: WHO certifies the Bahamas for eliminating mother-to-child transmission of HIV
entry.summary: In a landmark achievement for Caribbean public health, the World Health Organization (WHO) congratulates The Bahamas for becoming the latest Caribbean nation to be certified as having eliminated the mother-to-child transmission of HIV.

entry.title: The One Fruit Dietitians Recommend for Brain, Heart, and Gut Health
entry.summary: Reach for this nutrient-dense fruit the next time you're craving a healthy snack.

entry.title: What’s behind phantom ear ringing? Scientists find the brain switch
entry.summary: The findings point to a potential treatment for tinnitus, a sometimes debilitating condition affecting roughly one in 10 adults in the U.S.

entry.title: How does imagination really work in the brain? New explanation upends what we knew
entry.summary: Your brain is currently expending about a fifth of your body's energy, and almost none of that is being used for what you're doing right now. Reading these words, feeling the weight of your body in a chair—all of this together barely changes the rate at which…
